description_long: This is a medium-sized, open-topped wooden bucket or barrel with a polygonal (likely octagonal) cross-section. The exterior is finished in a light wood grain, and the structure is reinforced with thin, metallic vertical and horizontal bands at the edges and base. Two prominent metal side handles are attached near the rim, allowing for easy lifting and carrying. The interior appears to be the same wood material as the exterior, with no visible lining. The object is sturdy and utilitarian, suitable for holding liquids, tools, or other materials in an urban or garden setting.
description: A light wood-grain bucket or barrel with metal side handles and thin metal reinforcement bands, open at the top and polygonal in shape.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, and both metal side handles clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one metal handle and the polygonal wood structure, with the handle's attachment more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing both handles but from the opposite side, with wood grain and metal bands visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, again showing a single handle and the polygonal wood construction.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: thin metal vertical and base bands
